entry_rationale: STRONGLY FAVORED: NO on the $91 to $91.99 band for Mar. 13, 2026 was the least-risk remaining Tier 4 candidate because current WTI futures were about $94.42 and this contract only loses if Friday’s official front-month settlement lands inside that exact $1 band. Supporting Evidence: Investing.com showed WTI futures at $94.42 with a same-day range of $88.89 to $95.96, so the target band sat roughly $2.4 to $3.4 below current pricing, while the provided Kalshi market data already priced NO at 0.96 and YES at 0.05. Resolution Source: Coinbase’s event page states that the Mar. 13, 2026 WTI event is outcome-verified by ICE, providing a clear mechanical settlement source for the front-month settle price. Risk Checklist: passed clear resolution source, no formal appeals or review path, and strong position via narrow-band structure plus current price cushion; recent corroboration is available from live WTI pricing today. Risk Level: LOW; this market was selected as the single lowest-risk available candidate. Remaining Risks: oil remains headline-sensitive and Friday settlement could still move into 91.xx, but no formal challenge or discretionary-resolution risk was identified. Sources: https://www.coinbase.com/en-in/predictions/event/KXWTIW-26MAR13 ; https://www.investing.com/commodities/crude-oil . citeturn8search3turn4search1
